title: agropyron caninum subsp. subtriflorum
intro: Agropyron caninum subsp. subtriflorum, also known as a subspecies of the Elymus caninus plant, is a type of flowering grass native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant's medicinal properties include its ability to be used as a diuretic and to treat digestive issues, making it useful in treating conditions such as urinary tract infections and diarrhea. The health benefits of Agropyron caninum subsp. subtriflorum include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of Agropyron caninum subsp. subtriflorum can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use.
